title: Uncaught JSON parse errors in show/done commands expose raw Node.js stack traces
risk_statement: The tapShow, tapDone, actShow, meoShow, specShow, and mistakeShow command handlers called fs.readJsonSync() without try/catch. A malformed or adversarially crafted artifact JSON file would cause an unhandled SyntaxError or TypeError to surface as a raw Node.js stack trace, potentially revealing internal file paths, dependency versions, and call stack structure to an observer of CLI output.

acceptance.reason: Risk mitigated in v2.2.0: all 6 show/done handlers wrap fs.readJsonSync() in try/catch and display a user-friendly error message instead of a stack trace.

mitigations.detail: All 6 affected handlers (tapShow, tapDone, actShow, meoShow, specShow, mistakeShow) wrap fs.readJsonSync() in try/catch. On parse failure, process exits with code 1 and prints a user-friendly message: '❌ Could not parse {id}: file may be corrupted'.
